title: High Regards
content:
How much more prolific,
 of a writer,
 I could become?

If only advanced by age?

I searched longingly
 for some poetry to indulge in,
and what I found
 wasn't satisfactory.

These poets,
 albeit literate and eloquent,

 lacked abstraction.

Lacking the romance that writing brings.
Rooted solely in events,
 poetry inspired by experience,

rather than for expression of misunderstanding.

And I found this to be disappointing.
Such writers are heralded as great,
 who acclaimed?
Perhaps I am lacking in the richness of my perception,
but these writers of acclaim fall short;

Of my artistic glory.

title: Ebb Tide
content:
In my youth,
 I thought it wise to mark my name
 on the face of the Earth;

 In my adolescence
 I thought it better to mark my name
 in the stars;

 In my present
 I find it best to mark my name
 in those hearts.

 The Earth will be consumed,
 the stars will become dim.
 The hearts of those will not let go,

 until the brink sets in.

 Perhaps it is narcissistic
 to wish for such grandiose of a legacy…
 But man inherently strives for such.
 I am no exception.

 I have stared longingly into space,
 just as I have my lover’s;
 Longing all the same…

 to be remembered.

 For who am I without my memories?
 My trials and struggles;
 who am I without a thought to my being?
 Though I recognize I am more than that;

 my soul seeks something more than that.

 “Like a tide at its ebb,
 I’m at peace in the web of your arms.”
 But I never am,

 and I never will be.
